data.text:
Plan file: .kilo/plans/1783600489582-baz-marketing-site-audit.md
Read this file first and treat it as the source of truth for implementation.

## Handover from Planning Session

## Discoveries
- `middleware.ts` short-circuits auth at lines 14–15: if a `baz_session` cookie *exists at all*, it returns `NextResponse.next()` **without validating the token**. A forged/expired session cookie could bypass the gate. Protected paths are `/admin`, `/console`, `/hub`, `/portal`, `/dashboard`. Must verify each protected server component re-validates via `readSessionFromCookies()` (lib/auth.ts:53) AND checks `user.role`. Note: custom session (`baz_session`, httpOnly) and Supabase session are two parallel auth mechanisms — middleware checks the custom cookie; Supabase client is used in `/login` flows (per git history: "hardcoded Supabase fallback in /login client").
- Rate limiter (`lib/rate-limit.ts`) is an in-memory `Map` with a `setInterval` prune. On Vercel serverless this resets per-instance and is not shared → limits effectively unenforced in prod. It also trusts spoofable `x-forwarded-for`/`x-real-ip` (no fallback to user id). `DEFAULT_LIMITS` and per-route limits exist but are advisory only.
- `/api/score` is `POST`-only (`app/api/score/route.ts`) but `quality-inspect.mjs` line 63 GETs it → 405. The route is used by `LiveAgentDemo` for live lead scoring. Inspector treats only non-2xx/non-401 as failures; a POST-only route being GET'd is a false-positive that should be fixed in the inspector (skip POST-only routes) OR a GET added.
- Dual data layer: local `better-sqlite3` (`lib/db.ts`, `lib/db/pg-worker.js`, `data/baz.db`) appears to be the *live* store for leads/sessions/users/audit (auth.ts, leads route use `getDb()`), while Supabase (`lib/supabase-client.ts`, `lib/db/supabase.ts`) is used for auth middleware + RLS tables. `better-sqlite3` is a native module — risk on Vercel serverless builds. `leadScoring` LLM background call gated by `LEAD_SCORING !== "off"`.
- Inspector (`scripts/quality-inspect.mjs`) defines its OWN page/API lists (lines 37–64) that do **not** match actual routes (e.g. lists `/hub`, `/hub/cockpit`, `/hub/nova`, `/marketing-hub`, `/loop`, `/pulse`, `/portal/client`, `/console`, `/dashboard`, `/admin` — some may 404; lists `/api/ai`, `/api/auth/me`, `/api/leads`, `/api/feedback`, `/api/agents`, `/api/search` but actual api dir has more: `apps`, `books`, `cron`, `data`, `lead-portal`, `supabase-demo`). The inspector's stats also diverge from reality (it counted 60 pages/66 components but actual tree shows 88 component files + 51 app files). Treat inspector numbers as stale.
- `setAuthCookies()` (`lib/auth.ts:86`) sets `baz_user` as client-readable (httpOnly:false) — acceptable but note; session cookie is httpOnly+secure(prod).

## Relevant Files
- `/home/uzer/baz/middleware.ts` — auth gate, suspected bypass at lines 14–15.
- `/home/uzer/baz/lib/auth.ts` — cookie session store, `readSessionFromCookies`, `setAuthCookies`, `readUserCookie`.
- `/home/uzer/baz/lib/rate-limit.ts` — in-memory limiter, prod-unsafe.
- `/home/uzer/baz/app/api/score/route.ts` — POST-only; inspector GET → 405.
- `/home/uzer/baz/app/api/leads/route.ts` — GET/PATCH require `user` (any role), POST public + sqlite insert + scoring.
- `/home/uzer/baz/lib/supabase-client.ts` — server/ admin/ anon clients; `SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY` admin only server-side.
- `/home/uzer/baz/supabase/migrations/00002_rls_policies.sql`, `00003_*`, `00004_*` — RLS; verify anon publishable key can't read protected tables.
- `/home/uzer/baz/content/testimonials.ts` — 9 `placeholder: true` flags (known issue).
- `/home/uzer/baz/content/_NEW_TESTIMONIALS.ts` — likely duplicate/unused testimonial set.
- `/home/uzer/baz/scripts/quality-inspect.mjs` — inspect script; its route lists are stale vs actual tree.
- `/home/uzer/baz/app/layout.tsx` — CSS import order must be globals → aether-theme → aether-monochrome → color-layer (last).
- `/home/uzer/baz/app/color-layer.css`, `contrast-layer.css` — seed-driven recolor + forced-dark contrast.
- `/home/uzer/baz/lib/db.ts`, `lib/db/pg-worker.js`, `data/baz.db` — live sqlite store (leads/sessions/users).
- `/home/uzer/baz/components/primitives/*` (Modal, Toast, Tooltip, ContextMenu) — a11y review targets.
- `/home/uzer/baz/app/api/ai/route.ts` — confirm not an open LLM proxy (key leak / SSRF / injection).
- `/home/uzer/baz/quality-report.md`, `quality-report.json` — stale (2026-06-29) baseline.

## Implementation Notes
- This is a **read-only audit**; the implementing/audit agent should write findings to `AUDIT.md` (root), NOT modify source unless authorized to fix. Critical items (if confirmed) need a separate authorized fix pass.
- `npm run inspect` requires dev server on `:3000` first (`npm run dev`). The inspector is itself buggy (stale route lists, false 405) — when fixing, align route lists with the real tree and skip POST-only routes.
- Verify which auth is authoritative: custom `baz_session` (sqlite) vs Supabase. `middleware` trusts custom cookie presence only — the real protection may be per-page `requireUser()`/role checks; confirm before declaring a true bypass.
- `better-sqlite3` native dep + `data/baz.db` (with `-wal`/`-shm`) in repo — check `.gitignore`/`.dockerignore` and Vercel build compatibility before relying on it.
- Secrets: verify `.env.local` is gitignored (not committed); `.env.example` must contain no real values; confirm no secret reaches client bundle (grep for `SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY`, LLM/Canva keys in `lib/llm.ts`, `lib/canva.ts`).
- Duplicate animation libs: both `framer-motion@^12` and `motion@^12` are dependencies — consolidation opportunity, watch bundle size.
- AGENTS.md says "No gold/accent color anywhere until expert sets seed in color-layer.css" — design-system check should confirm no hardcoded hex and that color comes only from the 4-layer CSS + `color-layer.css` `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat`.

Let me analyze the plan file carefully. This is a read-only audit task for a marketing site. The implementer should write findings to `AUDIT.md` at the root. Let me understand the scope and begin the systematic audit.

1. Auth security (middleware bypass, Supabase vs custom session)
2. Rate limiting (in-memory, prod-unsafe)
3. /api/score route mismatch with inspector
4. Dual data layer (sqlite + supabase)

12. Component a11y review

**Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT  
**Design system source of truth:** `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` (read it first).

## Project Setup
- GitHub repo: `baz-marketing-site` (remote: `origin`)

- **B&W by default.** Color is a single expert-editable layer: `app/color-layer.css` (imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx` → always wins). Set `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat` there to recolor the whole site (e.g. cyan `187/90%`, gold `42/85%`). One file, two numbers.

- **Tokens, not hexes:** never hardcode color in components — use semantic Tailwind classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`) → tokens → `color-layer.css`.

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { type NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
2: import { createServerClient, type CookieOptions } from "@supabase/ssr";
3: 
4: const SUPABASE_URL = process.env.SUPABASE_URL!;
5: const SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY = process.env.SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!;
6: const PROTECTED_PATHS = ["/admin", "/console", "/hub", "/portal", "/dashboard"];
7: 
8: export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) {
9:   const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;
10: 
11:   const needsAuth = PROTECTED_PATHS.some((p) => pathname.startsWith(p));
12:   if (!needsAuth) return NextResponse.next();
13: 
14:   const customSession = req.cookies.get("baz_session")?.value;
15:   if (customSession) return NextResponse.next();
16: 
17:   let supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({ request: req });
18: 
19:   const supabase = createServerClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, {
20:     cookies: {
21:       get(name: string) {
22:         return req.cookies.get(name)?.value;
23:       },
24:       set(name: string, value: string, options: CookieOptions) {
25:         req.cookies.set({ name, value, ...options });
26:         supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({ request: req });
27:         supabaseResponse.cookies.set({ name, value, ...options });
28:       },
29:       remove(name: string, options: CookieOptions) {
30:         req.cookies.set({ name, value: "", ...options });
31:         supabaseResponse = NextResponse.next({ request: req });
32:         supabaseResponse.cookies.set({ name, value: "", ...options });
33:       },
34:     },
35:   });
36: 
37:   const { data: { session } } = await supabase.auth.getSession();
38: 
39:   if (!session) {
40:     const loginUrl = new URL("/login", req.url);
41:     loginUrl.searchParams.set("next", pathname);
42:     return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
43:   }
44: 
45:   return supabaseResponse;
46: }
47: 
48: export const config = {
49:   matcher: ["/admin/:path*", "/console/:path*", "/hub/:path*", "/portal/:path*", "/dashboard/:path*"],
50: };

(End of file - total 50 lines)
</content>

# BAZventures — Next.js + Supabase + Vercel

**Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT  
**Design system source of truth:** `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` (read it first).

## Project Setup
- GitHub repo: `baz-marketing-site` (remote: `origin`)
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime, Storage, Edge Functions)
- Vercel (Analytics, Speed Insights, OG Images, Cron, Edge Runtime)
- CI/CD via GitHub Actions (test → deploy preview → deploy production)

## Design — Black & White Color System
- **B&W by default.** Color is a single expert-editable layer: `app/color-layer.css` (imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx` → always wins). Set `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat` there to recolor the whole site (e.g. cyan `187/90%`, gold `42/85%`). One file, two numbers.
- **Dark mode only** — `ThemeProvider` forces `dark` (`forcedTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem={false}`). Background/text are seed-driven neutrals (currently greyscale).
- **4-layer CSS stack:** `globals.css` → `aether-theme.css` → `aether-monochrome.css` → `color-layer.css` (last wins). Full map in `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`.
- **Fonts:** Outfit (display), Poppins (body), JetBrains Mono (code/metrics).
- **Square corners everywhere** (`* { border-radius: 0 }`); `rounded-full` only for pills/badges.
- **Tokens, not hexes:** never hardcode color in components — use semantic Tailwind classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`) → tokens → `color-layer.css`.

## Brand Assets (B&W)
- **Favicon:** `public/favicon.svg` — B&W mark (dark squircle, light "B").
- **Logo (live):** a **text wordmark** rendered in `components/layout/Header.tsx` + `Footer.tsx` — a `bg-foreground` "B" square + "BAZventures" in Outfit. B&W, token-driven, auto-recolors via `--brand` when the seed is set.
- **Legacy logo SVGs** in `public/logo/` (`baz-mark*.svg`, `baz-wordmark*.svg`) are retained for the `/brandbook` reference page but are **not** used by the live header/footer.
- **OG images:** `/og`, `/stance-og`, `/case-studies-og`, `/methodology-og` — neutral palette, "BAZventures" wordmark.
- **No gold/accent color** anywhere on the public site until the expert sets the seed in `color-layer.css`.

## Key Scripts
- `npm run dev` — local dev (port 3000)
- `npm run build` — production build
- `npm test` — Playwright E2E tests
- `npm run supabase:types` — regenerate TS types from Supabase
- `npm run supabase:migrate` — push migrations to Supabase
- `npm run deploy` — deploy to Vercel

## Supabase
- Project: `uyqgmdrzyapbbvmaumvk.supabase.co`
- Migrations: `supabase/migrations/`
- Edge Functions: `supabase/functions/`
- Types: `lib/database.types.ts`
- Client: `lib/supabase-client.ts` (SSR + Admin + Anonymous)
- Realtime: `lib/realtime.ts` (client-side hooks)

## Vercel
- Project: `baz-marketing-site-y6bq` (team: brahimamirzerbout-9700s-projects)
- Analytics + Speed Insights in layout
- Dynamic OG images at `/og`
- Cron jobs: sitemap (daily), audit (weekly)
- Edge runtime: `/api/services`, `/api/health`

## CI/CD (GitHub Actions)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- PR → typecheck + lint + deploy preview + E2E tests
- Push to main → deploy production + Supabase migrations + health check

<path>/home/uzer/baz/lib/auth.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * BAZventures — Auth layer
3:  *
4:  * Cookie-based session auth (not JWT) so admin pages can read the session
5:  * server-side without round-tripping the API. Token in `baz_session` httpOnly
6:  * cookie; user info cached in `baz_user` cookie (readable by client code).
7:  *
8:  * Endpoints (see /api/auth/*):
9:  *   POST /api/auth/register  { email, password, name }   → issues session
10:  *   POST /api/auth/login     { email, password }         → issues session
11:  *   POST /api/auth/logout                              → clears session
12:  *   GET  /api/auth/me                                  → returns current user
13:  */
14: 
15: import { cookies } from "next/headers";
16: import { getDb, audit, id, hashPassword, verifyPassword, token } from "./db";
17: import type { User } from "@/types/auth";
18: 
19: const COOKIE_SESSION = "baz_session";
20: const COOKIE_USER = "baz_user";
21: const SESSION_TTL_DAYS = 30;
22: 
23: export interface SessionRecord {
24:   id: string;
25:   user_id: string;
26:   token: string;
27:   expires_at: number;
28:   created_at: number;
29: }
30: 
31: export async function createSession(userId: string): Promise<string> {
32:   const db = getDb();
33:   const sessionToken = token();
34:   const expiresAt = Date.now() + SESSION_TTL_DAYS * 86400 * 1000;
35:   db.prepare("INSERT INTO sessions (id, user_id, token, expires_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)").run(
36:     id("s"),
37:     userId,
38:     sessionToken,
39:     expiresAt,
40:   );
41:   audit(userId, "session.create", sessionToken);
42:   return sessionToken;
43: }
44: 
45: export async function destroySession(sessionToken: string): Promise<void> {
46:   const db = getDb();
47:   const session = db.prepare("SELECT user_id FROM sessions WHERE token = ?").get(sessionToken) as
48:     { user_id: string } | undefined;
49:   db.prepare("DELETE FROM sessions WHERE token = ?").run(sessionToken);
50:   if (session) audit(session.user_id, "session.destroy", sessionToken);
51: }
52: 
53: export async function readSessionFromCookies(): Promise<{
54:   user: User | null;
55:   token: string | null;
56: }> {
57:   const c = cookies();
58:   const token = c.get(COOKIE_SESSION)?.value ?? null;
59:   if (!token) return { user: null, token: null };
60:   const db = getDb();
61:   const session = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE token = ?").get(token) as
62:     SessionRecord | undefined;
63:   if (!session || session.expires_at < Date.now()) {
64:     if (session) db.prepare("DELETE FROM sessions WHERE token = ?").run(token);
65:     return { user: null, token: null };
66:   }
67:   const user = db
68:     .prepare("SELECT id, email, name, role, team, initials, color FROM users WHERE id = ?")
69:     .get(session.user_id) as User | undefined;
70:   return { user: user ?? null, token };
71: }

73: /** Read the current user synchronously from the user-cookie mirror. Used in
74:  *  client components that need a quick hint (display name, role). For
75:  *  authoritative user data, server components should call `requireUser()`. */
76: export function readUserCookie(): User | null {
77:   const raw = cookies().get(COOKIE_USER)?.value;
78:   if (!raw) return null;
79:   try {
80:     return JSON.parse(decodeURIComponent(raw)) as User;
81:   } catch {
82:     return null;
83:   }
84: }
85: 
86: export function setAuthCookies(sessionToken: string, user: User) {
87:   const c = cookies();
88:   const userJson = encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(user));
89:   c.set(COOKIE_SESSION, sessionToken, {
90:     httpOnly: true,
91:     secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
92:     sameSite: "lax",
93:     path: "/",
94:     maxAge: SESSION_TTL_DAYS * 86400,
95:   });
96:   c.set(COOKIE_USER, userJson, {
97:     httpOnly: false,
98:     secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production",
99:     sameSite: "lax",
100:     path: "/",
101:     maxAge: SESSION_TTL_DAYS * 86400,
102:   });
103: }
104: 
105: export function clearAuthCookies() {
106:   const c = cookies();
107:   c.delete(COOKIE_SESSION);
108:   c.delete(COOKIE_USER);
109: }
110: 
111: export function hashUserPassword(plain: string): string {
112:   return hashPassword(plain);
113: }
114: export function verifyUserPassword(plain: string, hash: string): boolean {

1: import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
2: 
3: interface Bucket {
4:   count: number;
5:   resetAt: number;
6: }
7: 
8: const buckets = new Map<string, Bucket>();
9: 
10: const PRUNE_INTERVAL = 5 * 60_000;
11: if (typeof setInterval !== "undefined") {
12:   setInterval(() => {
13:     const now = Date.now();
14:     for (const [k, b] of buckets) if (b.resetAt < now) buckets.delete(k);
15:   }, PRUNE_INTERVAL).unref?.();
16: }
17: 
18: export interface RateLimitOpts {
19:   key: string;
20:   limit: number;
21:   windowMs: number;
22: }
23: 
24: export function rateLimit(
25:   req: Request,
26:   opts: RateLimitOpts,
27: ): { ok: true; remaining: number; resetAt: number } | { ok: false; retryAfter: number } {
28:   const ip =
29:     req.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ??
30:     req.headers.get("x-real-ip") ??
31:     "unknown";
32:   const k = `${opts.key}:${ip}`;
33:   const now = Date.now();
34:   const existing = buckets.get(k);
35: 
36:   if (!existing || existing.resetAt < now) {
37:     buckets.set(k, { count: 1, resetAt: now + opts.windowMs });
38:     return { ok: true, remaining: opts.limit - 1, resetAt: now + opts.windowMs };
39:   }
40: 
41:   if (existing.count >= opts.limit) {
42:     return { ok: false, retryAfter: Math.ceil((existing.resetAt - now) / 1000) };
43:   }
44: 
45:   existing.count += 1;
46:   return { ok: true, remaining: opts.limit - existing.count, resetAt: existing.resetAt };
47: }
48: 
49: export function rateLimitHeaders(
50:   result: { ok: true; remaining: number; resetAt: number } | { ok: false; retryAfter: number },
51: ): HeadersInit {
52:   const headers: Record<string, string> = {};
53:   if (result.ok) {
54:     headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"] = String(result.remaining);
55:     headers["X-RateLimit-Reset"] = String(Math.ceil(result.resetAt / 1000));
56:   } else {
57:     headers["Retry-After"] = String(result.retryAfter);
58:   }
59:   return headers;
60: }
61: 
62: export function rateLimitedResponse(result: { retryAfter: number }): NextResponse {
63:   return NextResponse.json(
64:     { ok: false, error: "rate_limited" },
65:     {
66:       status: 429,
67:       headers: {
68:         "Retry-After": String(result.retryAfter),
69:         "X-RateLimit-Reset": String(Math.ceil((Date.now() + result.retryAfter * 1000) / 1000)),
70:       },
71:     },
72:   );
73: }
74: 
75: export const DEFAULT_LIMITS: Record<string, { limit: number; windowMs: number }> = {
76:   "contact-form": { limit: 5, windowMs: 60_000 },
77:   "agent-run": { limit: 20, windowMs: 60_000 },
78:   "auth-login": { limit: 10, windowMs: 60_000 },
79:   "auth-register": { limit: 3, windowMs: 60_000 },
80:   "api-leads": { limit: 30, windowMs: 60_000 },
81:   "search": { limit: 60, windowMs: 60_000 },
82:   "feedback": { limit: 10, windowMs: 60_000 },
83: };

(End of file - total 83 lines)
</content>

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: /**
3:  * BAZ Quality Inspector — comprehensive site audit.
4:  *
5:  * Checks:
6:  *   1. All pages return HTTP 200
7:  *   2. All API routes respond
8:  *   3. Placeholder content flags
9:  *   4. Broken internal links
10:  *   5. Missing SEO meta tags
11:  *   6. Console errors (via HTML inspection)
12:  *   7. Accessibility issues
13:  *   8. Performance signals
14:  *
15:  * Outputs: quality-report.json + quality-report.md
16:  */
17: 
18: import { readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync, writeFileSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs';
19: import { join, extname, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path';
20: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
21: 
22: const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
23: const ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..');
24: const SITE = process.env.SITE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';
25: 
26: const results = {
27:   timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
28:   site: SITE,
29:   checks: {},
30:   issues: [],
31:   passed: [],
32:   stats: {},
33: };
34: 
35: // ─── 1. PAGE STATUS CHECK ─────────────────────────────────
36: async function checkPages() {
37:   const pages = [
38:     '/', '/hub', '/hub/cockpit', '/hub/triangle', '/hub/nova',
39:     '/services', '/case-studies', '/insights', '/pricing', '/about',
40:     '/contact', '/methodology', '/our-story', '/stance', '/vs-others',
41:     '/marketing-hub', '/brandbook', '/become-an-operator',
42:     '/loop', '/pulse', '/portal', '/portal/client',
43:     '/login', '/signup', '/dashboard', '/console', '/admin',
44:   ];
45:   const ok = []; const fail = [];
46:   for (const p of pages) {
47:     try {
48:       const r = await fetch(`${SITE}${p}`, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) });
49:       if (r.ok) ok.push(p); else fail.push({ path: p, status: r.status });
50:     } catch (e) {
51:       fail.push({ path: p, status: 'timeout' });
52:     }
53:   }
54:   results.checks.pages = { passed: ok.length, failed: fail.length, failures: fail };
55:   if (fail.length === 0) results.passed.push(`All ${ok.length} pages return 200`);
56:   else fail.forEach(f => results.issues.push(`Page ${f.path} returned ${f.status}`));
57: }
58: 
59: // ─── 2. API ROUTE CHECK ───────────────────────────────────
60: async function checkApis() {
61:   const routes = [
62:     '/api/health', '/api/services', '/api/ai', '/api/auth/me',
63:     '/api/leads', '/api/feedback', '/api/agents', '/api/search', '/api/score',
64:   ];
65:   const ok = []; const fail = [];
66:   for (const r of routes) {
67:     try {
68:       const res = await fetch(`${SITE}${r}`, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) });
69:       // 401 is OK for auth-protected routes
70:       if (res.ok || res.status === 401) ok.push(r);
71:       else fail.push({ route: r, status: res.status });
72:     } catch (e) {
73:       fail.push({ route: r, status: 'timeout' });
74:     }
75:   }
76:   results.checks.apis = { passed: ok.length, failed: fail.length, failures: fail };
77:   if (fail.length === 0) results.passed.push(`All ${ok.length} API routes respond`);
78:   else fail.forEach(f => results.issues.push(`API ${f.route} returned ${f.status}`));
79: }
80: 
81: // ─── 3. PLACEHOLDER AUDIT ─────────────────────────────────
82: function checkPlaceholders() {
83:   const SCAN_DIRS = ['app', 'components', 'content'];
84:   const SCAN_EXTS = new Set(['.ts', '.tsx', '.js', '.jsx', '.mdx', '.md']);
85:   const SKIP_DIRS = new Set(['node_modules', '.next', '.git', 'dist', 'build']);
86:   const findings = [];
87: 
88:   function walk(dir) {
89:     try {
90:       for (const entry of readdirSync(dir)) {
91:         if (SKIP_DIRS.has(entry)) continue;
92:         const p = join(dir, entry);
93:         const s = statSync(p);
94:         if (s.isDirectory()) walk(p);
95:         else if (SCAN_EXTS.has(extname(p))) scan(p);
96:       }
97:     } catch {}
98:   }
99: 
100:   function scan(p) {
101:     const txt = readFileSync(p, 'utf8');
102:     if (/placeholder:\s*true/.test(txt)) {
103:       const matches = txt.match(/placeholder:\s*true/g);
104:       findings.push({ file: p, count: matches?.length || 0 });
105:     }
106:     if (/\bTODO\b/.test(txt)) {
107:       const matches = txt.match(/\bTODO\b/g);
108:       findings.push({ file: p, type: 'TODO', count: matches?.length || 0 });
109:     }
110:   }
111: 
112:   for (const d of SCAN_DIRS) walk(join(ROOT, d));
113:   results.checks.placeholders = { total: findings.length, findings };
114:   const placeholderCount = findings.filter(f => f.count && !f.type).length;
115:   if (placeholderCount === 0) results.passed.push('No placeholder flags found');
116:   else results.issues.push(`${placeholderCount} files with placeholder: true flags`);
117: }
118: 
119: // ─── 4. SEO CHECK ─────────────────────────────────────────
120: async function checkSeo() {
121:   const checks = [];
122:   const pages = ['/', '/services', '/case-studies', '/insights', '/pricing', '/about'];
123: 
124:   for (const p of pages) {
125:     try {
126:       const html = await fetch(`${SITE}${p}`).then(r => r.text());
127:       const has = (re) => re.test(html);
128:       checks.push({
129:         path: p,
130:         title: has(/<title>[^<]+<\/title>/),
131:         description: has(/<meta name="description"/),
132:         ogTitle: has(/property="og:title"/),
133:         ogImage: has(/property="og:image"/),
134:         canonical: has(/rel="canonical"/),
135:         jsonLd: has(/application\/ld\+json/),
136:       });
137:     } catch {
138:       checks.push({ path: p, error: 'fetch failed' });
139:     }
140:   }
141: 
142:   const allGood = checks.every(c => c.title && c.description && c.ogTitle && c.canonical);
143:   results.checks.seo = { pages: checks.length, allPass: allGood, details: checks };
144:   if (allGood) results.passed.push(`SEO meta tags present on all ${checks.length} checked pages`);
145:   else checks.filter(c => !c.title || !c.description || !c.ogTitle).forEach(c =>
146:     results.issues.push(`SEO incomplete on ${c.path}`)
147:   );
148: }
149: 
150: // ─── 5. FILE STATS ────────────────────────────────────────
151: function checkFiles() {
152:   const stats = {
153:     components: 0, pages: 0, apiRoutes: 0, contentFiles: 0,
154:     libFiles: 0, totalLines: 0, beuiComponents: 0,
155:   };
156: 
157:   function countDir(dir, key, ext = '.tsx') {
158:     try {
159:       for (const entry of readdirSync(join(ROOT, dir))) {
160:         if (entry === 'node_modules' || entry === '.next') continue;
161:         const p = join(join(ROOT, dir), entry);
162:         try {
163:           if (statSync(p).isDirectory()) {
164:             function walk(d) {
165:               try {
166:                 for (const e of readdirSync(d)) {
167:                   if (SKIP.has(e)) continue;
168:                   const fp = join(d, e);
169:                   if (statSync(fp).isDirectory()) walk(fp);
170:                   else if (e.endsWith(ext)) stats[key]++;
171:                   else if (e.endsWith('.ts') || e.endsWith('.tsx')) {
172:                     try { stats.totalLines += readFileSync(fp, 'utf8').split('\n').length; } catch {}
173:                   }
174:                 }
175:               } catch {}
176:             }
177:             walk(p);
178:           } else if (entry.endsWith(ext)) stats[key]++;
179:         } catch {}
180:       }
181:     } catch {}
182:   }
183: 
184:   const SKIP = new Set(['node_modules', '.next', '.git', 'dist', 'build']);
185:   countDir('components', 'components');
186:   countDir('app', 'pages');
187:   countDir('app/api', 'apiRoutes', '.ts');
188:   countDir('content', 'contentFiles', '.ts');
189:   countDir('lib', 'libFiles', '.ts');
190: 
191:   // Count beUI
192:   try {
193:     stats.beuiComponents = readdirSync(join(ROOT, 'components/beui')).filter(f => f.endsWith('.tsx')).length;
194:   } catch {}
195: 
196:   results.stats = stats;
197:   results.passed.push(`${stats.components} components, ${stats.pages} pages, ${stats.apiRoutes} API routes`);
198: }
199: 
200: // ─── 6. DESIGN SYSTEM CHECK ───────────────────────────────
201: function checkDesign() {
202:   const css = readFileSync(join(ROOT, 'app/globals.css'), 'utf8');
203:   const checks = {
204:     goldRules: (css.match(/gold/g) || []).length,
205:     hasDarkMode: /\[data-theme='dark'\]/.test(css),
206:     hasRoyalSeal: /\.royal-seal/.test(css),
207:     hasGoldCursor: /var\(--gold-500\)/.test(css),
208:     hasScrollProgress: /\.scroll-progress/.test(css),
209:     hasHairlineGold: /\.hairline-gold/.test(css),
210:     hasBtnGold: /\.btn-gold/.test(css),
211:     hasEyebrowGold: /\.eyebrow-gold/.test(css),
212:     hasGrain: /\.grain/.test(css),
213:     hasRoyalEntrance: /\.royal-entrance/.test(css),
214:   };
215:   results.checks.design = checks;
216:   const goldCount = Object.values(checks).filter(Boolean).length;
217:   results.passed.push(`Design system: ${goldCount}/${Object.keys(checks).length} royal features present`);
218: }
219: 
220: // ─── RUN ALL ──────────────────────────────────────────────
221: async function main() {
222:   console.log('🔍 BAZ Quality Inspector — scanning...\n');
223: 
224:   await checkPages();
225:   await checkApis();
226:   checkPlaceholders();
227:   await checkSeo();
228:   checkFiles();
229:   checkDesign();
230: 
231:   // Summary
232:   const totalIssues = results.issues.length;
233:   const totalPassed = results.passed.length;
234: 
235:   console.log(`\n📊 QUALITY REPORT`);
236:   console.log(`══════════════════════════════════════════════════`);
237:   console.log(`✅ Passed: ${totalPassed}`);
238:   console.log(`⚠️  Issues: ${totalIssues}`);
239:   console.log(`══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n`);
240: 
241:   results.passed.forEach(p => console.log(`  ✅ ${p}`));
242:   results.issues.forEach(i => console.log(`  ⚠️  ${i}`));
243: 
244:   console.log(`\n📁 File Stats:`);
245:   Object.entries(results.stats).forEach(([k, v]) => console.log(`  ${k}: ${v}`));
246: 
247:   // Write reports
248:   writeFileSync(join(ROOT, 'quality-report.json'), JSON.stringify(results, null, 2));
249:   
250:   const md = generateMarkdown(results);
251:   writeFileSync(join(ROOT, 'quality-report.md'), md);
252: 
253:   console.log(`\n📦 Reports saved: quality-report.json, quality-report.md`);
254:   
255:   process.exit(totalIssues > 0 ? 0 : 0); // Don't fail CI in dev
256: }
257: 
258: function generateMarkdown(r) {
259:   return `# BAZ Quality Report
260: > Generated: ${r.timestamp}
261: 
262: ## Summary
263: - **Passed**: ${r.passed.length}
264: - **Issues**: ${r.issues.length}
265: 
266: ## Checks
267: 
268: ### Pages
269: - Passed: ${r.checks.pages?.passed || 0}
270: - Failed: ${r.checks.pages?.failed || 0}
271: ${(r.checks.pages?.failures || []).map(f => `- ❌ ${f.path}: ${f.status}`).join('\n')}
272: 
273: ### API Routes
274: - Passed: ${r.checks.apis?.passed || 0}
275: - Failed: ${r.checks.apis?.failed || 0}
276: 
277: ### Placeholders
278: - Total flagged: ${r.checks.placeholders?.total || 0}
279: 
280: ### SEO
281: - All pages have meta tags: ${r.checks.seo?.allPass ? '✅' : '⚠️'}
282: 
283: ### Design System
284: ${Object.entries(r.checks.design || {}).map(([k, v]) => `- ${v ? '✅' : '❌'} ${k}`).join('\n')}
285: 
286: ## File Stats
287: ${Object.entries(r.stats).map(([k, v]) => `- ${k}: ${v}`).join('\n')}
288: 
289: ## Passed
290: ${r.passed.map(p => `- ✅ ${p}`).join('\n')}
291: 
292: ## Issues
293: ${r.issues.map(i => `- ⚠️ ${i}`).join('\n')}
294: `;
295: }
296: 
297: main().catch(console.error);

1: import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
2: import { getDb, audit, id } from "@/lib/db";
3: import { readSessionFromCookies } from "@/lib/auth";
4: import { rateLimit, rateLimitHeaders } from "@/lib/rate-limit";
5: 
6: export const runtime = "nodejs";
7: export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
8: 
9: /**
10:  * GET /api/leads — list leads. Auth required (operator view).
11:  * Query params: ?status=new|contacted|qualified|proposal|won|lost
12:  *              ?limit=N
13:  *              ?q=text (search)
14:  */
15: export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {
16:   const { user } = await readSessionFromCookies();
17:   if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" }, { status: 401 });
18: 
19:   const url = new URL(req.url);
20:   const status = url.searchParams.get("status");
21:   const limit = Math.min(parseInt(url.searchParams.get("limit") ?? "100", 10) || 100, 1000);
22:   const q = url.searchParams.get("q")?.trim();
23: 
24:   const db = getDb();
25:   const where: string[] = [];
26:   const args: (string | number)[] = [];
27:   if (status) {
28:     where.push("status = ?");
29:     args.push(status);
30:   }
31:   if (q) {
32:     where.push("(name LIKE ? OR email LIKE ? OR company LIKE ? OR message LIKE ?)");
33:     args.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`, `%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);
34:   }
35:   const sql = `SELECT * FROM leads ${where.length ? "WHERE " + where.join(" AND ") : ""} ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?`;
36:   args.push(limit);
37:   const leads = db.prepare(sql).all(...args);
38: 
39:   // Funnel summary
40:   const funnel = db
41:     .prepare(
42:       `
43:     SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM leads GROUP BY status
44:   `,
45:     )
46:     .all();
47: 
48:   return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, leads, funnel, total: leads.length });
49: }
50: 
51: /**
52:  * POST /api/leads — create a lead. Public (no auth required).
53:  * Used by the /contact form, the /brief form, and external integrations.
54:  */
55: export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
56:   // 10 leads / minute / IP — stops spam, allows legitimate bursts
57:   const guard = rateLimit(req, { key: "lead-create", limit: 10, windowMs: 60_000 });
58:   if (!guard.ok) {
59:     return NextResponse.json(
60:       { ok: false, error: "rate_limited" },
61:       { status: 429, headers: rateLimitHeaders(guard) },
62:     );
63:   }
64: 
65:   let body: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
66:   try {
67:     body = await req.json();
68:   } catch {
69:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "invalid_json" }, { status: 400 });
70:   }
71:   if (!body) return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "empty_body" }, { status: 400 });
72:   const name = (body.name || "").toString().trim();
73:   const email = (body.email || "").toString().trim().toLowerCase();
74:   const message = (body.message || "").toString().trim();
75:   if (!name || !email || !message)
76:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "missing_fields" }, { status: 400 });
77:   if (!/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email))
78:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "invalid_email" }, { status: 400 });
79: 
80:   const db = getDb();
81:   const leadId = id("l");
82:   const source = (body.source || "marketing_site").toString().slice(0, 64);
83:   const service = (body.service || "").toString().slice(0, 96);
84:   db.prepare(
85:     `INSERT INTO leads (id, name, email, company, website, phone, budget, message, source, service)
86:               VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
87:   ).run(
88:     leadId,
89:     name,
90:     email,
91:     body.company?.toString().slice(0, 200),
92:     body.website?.toString().slice(0, 500),
93:     body.phone?.toString().slice(0, 64),
94:     body.budget?.toString().slice(0, 64),
95:     message,
96:     source,
97:     service,
98:   );
99:   audit(null, "lead.create", leadId, { source, service });
100: 
101:   // Always run deterministic scoring — it's free and works without LLM.
102:   // Layer LLM scoring on top if configured.
103:   const { scoreLead } = await import("@/lib/scoring");
104:   const baseScore = scoreLead({
105:     message,
106:     budget: body.budget as string | undefined,
107:     company: body.company as string | undefined,
108:     website: body.website as string | undefined,
109:     source,
110:     service,
111:     demoCompleted: !!body.demoCompleted,
112:     agentRuns: typeof body.agentRuns === "number" ? body.agentRuns : undefined,
113:     timeOnPageSec: typeof body.timeOnPageSec === "number" ? body.timeOnPageSec : undefined,
114:     scrollDepth: typeof body.scrollDepth === "number" ? body.scrollDepth : undefined,
115:     isRepeat: !!body.isRepeat,
116:   });
117:   db.prepare("UPDATE leads SET score = ?, intent = ? WHERE id = ?").run(
118:     baseScore.score,
119:     baseScore.intent,
120:     leadId,
121:   );
122:   audit(null, "lead.score", leadId, {
123:     score: baseScore.score,
124:     intent: baseScore.intent,
125:     reasons: baseScore.reasons,
126:   });
127: 
128:   // Fire-and-forget: layer LLM scoring on top if available.
129:   if (process.env.LEAD_SCORING !== "off") {
130:     scoreLeadInBackground(leadId, message).catch(() => {});
131:   }
132: 
133:   return NextResponse.json({
134:     ok: true,
135:     id: leadId,
136:     score: baseScore.score,
137:     intent: baseScore.intent,
138:     action: baseScore.recommendedAction,
139:   });
140: }
141: 
142: async function scoreLeadInBackground(leadId: string, message: string) {
143:   const { complete } = await import("@/lib/llm");
144:   const result = await complete({
145:     prompt: `Refine the lead score (currently 0-100 baseline) based on this message. Return JSON {"adjust": <int between -20 and +20>, "intent": "<buy_now|researching|comparison_shopping|tire_kicker>"}.\\n\\nMessage:\\n${message.slice(0, 2000)}`,
146:     system: `You are BAZ LeadGen Agent. Adjust the deterministic lead score by -20 to +20 based on subtleties. Return strict JSON only.`,
147:     maxTokens: 200,
148:     temperature: 0.2,
149:   });
150:   if (!result.ok || !result.text) return;
151:   try {
152:     const parsed = JSON.parse(result.text.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/)?.[0] ?? "{}");
153:     const adjust = Math.max(-20, Math.min(20, parseInt(parsed.adjust, 10) || 0));
154:     const intent = String(parsed.intent || "").slice(0, 32);
155:     if (adjust !== 0 || intent) {
156:       const db = getDb();
157:       const row = db.prepare("SELECT score FROM leads WHERE id = ?").get(leadId) as
158:         { score: number } | undefined;
159:       const base = row?.score ?? 0;
160:       const final = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, base + adjust));
161:       db.prepare("UPDATE leads SET score = ?, intent = ? WHERE id = ?").run(
162:         final,
163:         intent || undefined,
164:         leadId,
165:       );
166:     }
167:   } catch {
168:     // ignore parse errors
169:   }
170: }
171: 
172: /**
173:  * PATCH /api/leads?id=X — update status / score / owner. Auth required.
174:  */
175: export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {
176:   const { user } = await readSessionFromCookies();
177:   if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "unauthenticated" }, { status: 401 });
178:   const url = new URL(req.url);
179:   const leadId = url.searchParams.get("id");
180:   if (!leadId) return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "missing_id" }, { status: 400 });
181: 
182:   const body = await req.json().catch(() => ({}));
183:   const db = getDb();
184:   const sets: string[] = [];
185:   const args: (string | number)[] = [];
186:   for (const field of ["status", "score", "owner"]) {
187:     if (field in body) {
188:       sets.push(`${field} = ?`);
189:       args.push(body[field]);
190:     }
191:   }
192:   if (sets.length === 0)
193:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "no_fields" }, { status: 400 });
194:   sets.push("updated_at = ?");
195:   args.push(Date.now());
196:   args.push(leadId);
197:   db.prepare(`UPDATE leads SET ${sets.join(", ")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...args);
198:   audit(user.id, "lead.update", leadId, body);
199:   return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });
200: }

(End of file - total 200 lines)
</content>

1: import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
2: import { complete, llmStatus } from "@/lib/llm";

4: export const runtime = "nodejs";
5: export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
6: 
7: /**
8:  * POST /api/ai
9:  *
10:  * Body: { prompt: string, system?: string, maxTokens?: number, temperature?: number }
11:  *
12:  * Provider-agnostic completion. Returns structured { ok, text, provider, model, error }.
13:  * If no AI provider is configured (no keys), returns ok: false with error 'no_provider_configured'
14:  * — the client should then fall back to its offline copy.
15:  *
16:  * Used by:
17:  *   - The homepage LiveAgentDemo (replaces the client-side stub)
18:  *   - /admin/leads (auto-summarizing a lead)
19:  *   - any future feature
20:  */
21: export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
22:   let raw: unknown;
23:   try {
24:     raw = await req.json();
25:   } catch {
26:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "invalid_json" }, { status: 400 });
27:   }
28: 
29:   const { prompt, system, maxTokens, temperature } = (raw ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
30:   if (typeof prompt !== "string" || prompt.trim().length === 0) {
31:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "missing_prompt" }, { status: 400 });
32:   }
33:   if (prompt.length > 8000) {
34:     return NextResponse.json({ ok: false, error: "prompt_too_long" }, { status: 400 });
35:   }
36: 
37:   const result = await complete({
38:     prompt: prompt.trim(),
39:     system: typeof system === "string" ? system : undefined,
40:     maxTokens: typeof maxTokens === "number" ? maxTokens : undefined,
41:     temperature: typeof temperature === "number" ? temperature : undefined,
42:   });
43: 
44:   // Log non-OK results for observability without leaking details to client
45:   if (!result.ok) {
46:     console.warn("[baz:ai]", result.error, { provider: result.provider });
47:   }
48: 
49:   return NextResponse.json(result);
50: }
51: 
52: /**
53:  * GET /api/ai — status check
54:  * Returns the active provider, model, and which keys are configured.
55:  * No auth required (informational only).
56:  */
57: export async function GET() {
58:   return NextResponse.json(llmStatus());
59: }

2: import { cookies } from "next/headers";
3: import { createClient as createSupabaseClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";
4: import type { Database } from "./database.types";
5: 
6: const SUPABASE_URL = process.env.SUPABASE_URL!;
7: const SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY = process.env.SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!;
8: const SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY = process.env.SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY!;
9: 
10: export function createClient(cookieStore: ReturnType<typeof cookies>) {
11:   return createServerClient<Database>(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, {
12:     cookies: {
13:       get(name: string) {
14:         return cookieStore.get(name)?.value;
15:       },
16:       set(name: string, value: string, options: CookieOptions) {
17:         try {
18:           cookieStore.set({ name, value, ...options });
19:         } catch {}
20:       },
21:       remove(name: string, options: CookieOptions) {
22:         try {
23:           cookieStore.set({ name, value: "", ...options });
24:         } catch {}
25:       },
26:     },
27:   });
28: }
29: 
30: export function createAdminClient() {

32:     auth: {

450: // ── In-memory fallback for serverless (Vercel) ──────────────
451: // A minimal SQL-ish store that supports the subset of operations
452: // the BAZventures API routes use. Data lives in RAM for the function
453: // instance lifetime (seconds to minutes on Vercel).
454: 
455: function createInMemoryDb(): SqliteDb {
456:   const tables: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>[]> = {};
457:   const pkIndex: Record<string, Record<string, number>> = {}; // table -> pkValue -> rowIndex
458: 
459:   function ensureTable(name: string) {
460:     if (!tables[name]) {
461:       tables[name] = [];
462:       pkIndex[name] = {};
463:     }
464:   }
465: 
466:   // Very small SQL parser — handles CREATE TABLE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE, PRAGMA, COUNT
467:   function exec(sql: string): void {
468:     const statements = sql.split(";").map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean);
469:     for (const stmt of statements) {
470:       const upper = stmt.toUpperCase();
471: 
472:       // CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <name> ( ... )
473:       const createMatch = stmt.match(/^CREATE\s+TABLE\s+IF\s+NOT\s+EXISTS\s+(\w+)/i);
474:       if (createMatch) {
475:         ensureTable(createMatch[1]);
476:         continue;
477:       }
478: 
479:       // CREATE INDEX ... (no-op in memory)
480:       if (/^CREATE\s+INDEX/i.test(stmt)) continue;
481: 
482:       // ALTER TABLE <name> ADD COLUMN <col> <type> (no-op — rows are dynamic objects)
483:       if (/^ALTER\s+TABLE/i.test(stmt)) continue;
484:     }
485:   }
486: 
487:   function prepare(sql: string) {
488:     return {
489:       run(...args: unknown[]): unknown {
490:         const { action, table, columns, values, where, pkValue } = parseSql(sql, args);
491:         ensureTable(table);
492: 
493:         if (action === "INSERT") {
494:           const row: Record<string, unknown> = {};
495:           columns.forEach((col, i) => { row[col] = values[i]; });
496:           // Auto-increment for INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
497:           tables[table].push(row);
498:           if (pkValue != null) pkIndex[table][String(pkValue)] = tables[table].length - 1;
499:           return { changes: 1, lastInsertRowid: tables[table].length };
500:         }
501: 
502:         if (action === "UPDATE") {
503:           let changes = 0;
504:           for (const row of tables[table]) {
505:             if (matchesWhere(row, where, args)) {
506:               columns.forEach((col, i) => { row[col] = values[i]; });
507:               changes++;
508:             }
509:           }
510:           return { changes };
511:         }
512: 
513:         if (action === "DELETE") {
514:           const before = tables[table].length;
515:           tables[table] = tables[table].filter((row) => !matchesWhere(row, where, args));
516:           return { changes: before - tables[table].length };
517:         }
518: 
519:         return { changes: 0 };
520:       },
521: 
522:       get(...args: unknown[]): unknown {
523:         const { action, table, where } = parseSql(sql, args);
524:         ensureTable(table);
525: 
526:         if (action === "SELECT") {
527:           // SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM <table> [WHERE ...]
528:           const countMatch = sql.match(/SELECT\s+COUNT\(\*\)\s+AS\s+(\w+)/i);
529:           if (countMatch) {
530:             const rows = tables[table].filter((row) => matchesWhere(row, where, args));
531:             return { [countMatch[1]]: rows.length };
532:           }
533:           // SELECT * FROM <table> WHERE col = ? [ORDER BY ... LIMIT ?]
534:           const rows = tables[table].filter((row) => matchesWhere(row, where, args));
535:           return rows[0] ?? undefined;
536:         }
537: 
538:         // PRAGMA table_info(<table>)
539:         const pragmaMatch = sql.match(/PRAGMA\s+table_info\((\w+)\)/i);
540:         if (pragmaMatch) {
541:           ensureTable(pragmaMatch[1]);
542:           const sample = tables[pragmaMatch[1]][0];
543:           if (!sample) return [];
544:           return Object.keys(sample).map((name) => ({ name }));
545:         }
546: 
547:         return undefined;
548:       },
549: 
550:       all(...args: unknown[]): unknown[] {
551:         const { action, table, where } = parseSql(sql, args);
552:         ensureTable(table);
553: 
554:         if (action === "SELECT") {
555:           let rows = tables[table].filter((row) => matchesWhere(row, where, args));
556:           // ORDER BY <col> DESC/ASC
557:           const orderMatch = sql.match(/ORDER\s+BY\s+(\w+)\s+(DESC|ASC)/i);
558:           if (orderMatch) {
559:             const col = orderMatch[1];
560:             const dir = orderMatch[2].toUpperCase();
561:             rows = [...rows].sort((a, b) => {
562:               const av = a[col] as number | string;
563:               const bv = b[col] as number | string;
564:               if (av < bv) return dir === "DESC" ? 1 : -1;
565:               if (av > bv) return dir === "DESC" ? -1 : 1;
566:               return 0;
567:             });
568:           }
569:           // LIMIT ?
570:           const limitMatch = sql.match(/LIMIT\s+\?/i);
571:           if (limitMatch) {
572:             const limitIdx = args.length - 1;
573:             const limit = Number(args[limitIdx]);
574:             if (limit > 0) rows = rows.slice(0, limit);
575:           }
576:           return rows;
577:         }
578: 
579:         // SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM <table> GROUP BY status
580:         const groupMatch = sql.match(/SELECT\s+(\w+),\s*COUNT\(\*\)\s+AS\s+\w+\s+FROM\s+(\w+)\s+GROUP\s+BY/i);
581:         if (groupMatch) {
582:           const col = groupMatch[1];
583:           ensureTable(groupMatch[2]);
584:           const groups: Record<string, number> = {};
585:           for (const row of tables[groupMatch[2]]) {
586:             const key = String(row[col] ?? "");
587:             groups[key] = (groups[key] ?? 0) + 1;
588:           }
589:           return Object.entries(groups).map(([k, n]) => ({ status: k, n }));
590:         }
591: 
592:         return [];
593:       },
594:     };
595:   }
596: 
597:   function pragma(str: string): unknown {
598:     // no-op for in-memory
599:     return undefined;
600:   }
601: 
602:   // Tiny SQL parser — extracts action, table, columns, values, where clause
603:   function parseSql(sql: string, args: unknown[]): {
604:     action: string;
605:     table: string;
606:     columns: string[];
607:     values: unknown[];
608:     where: { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] | null;
609:     pkValue: unknown;
610:   } {
611:     const trimmed = sql.trim();
612:     const upper = trimmed.toUpperCase();
613: 
614:     // INSERT INTO <table> (cols...) VALUES (?, ?, ...)
615:     const insertMatch = trimmed.match(/^INSERT\s+INTO\s+(\w+)\s*\(([^)]+)\)\s*VALUES\s*\(([^)]+)\)/i);
616:     if (insertMatch) {
617:       const table = insertMatch[1];
618:       const columns = insertMatch[2].split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
619:       const placeholders = insertMatch[3].split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
620:       const values = placeholders.map((_, i) => args[i]);
621:       // Find PK for index
622:       let pkValue: unknown = undefined;
623:       const pkCol = columns[0]; // convention: first col is PK
624:       pkValue = values[0];
625:       return { action: "INSERT", table, columns, values, where: null, pkValue };
626:     }
627: 
628:     // UPDATE <table> SET col = ?, col2 = ? WHERE col = ?
629:     const updateMatch = trimmed.match(/^UPDATE\s+(\w+)\s+SET\s+(.+?)\s+WHERE\s+(.+)/i);
630:     if (updateMatch) {
631:       const table = updateMatch[1];
632:       const setPart = updateMatch[2];
633:       const wherePart = updateMatch[3];
634:       const setCols = setPart.split(",").map((s) => s.trim().split("=")[0].trim());
635:       // Count SET placeholders + WHERE placeholders
636:       const setPlaceholders = (setPart.match(/\?/g) || []).length;
637:       const setValues = args.slice(0, setPlaceholders);
638:       const whereArgs = args.slice(setPlaceholders);
639:       const where = parseWhereClause(wherePart, 0);
640:       // Shift where arg indices
641:       const adjustedWhere = where?.map((w) => ({ ...w, argIdx: w.argIdx + setPlaceholders })) ?? null;
642:       return { action: "UPDATE", table, columns: setCols, values: setValues, where: adjustedWhere, pkValue: undefined };
643:     }
644: 
645:     // DELETE FROM <table> WHERE col = ?
646:     const deleteMatch = trimmed.match(/^DELETE\s+FROM\s+(\w+)\s+WHERE\s+(.+)/i);
647:     if (deleteMatch) {
648:       const where = parseWhereClause(deleteMatch[2], 0);
649:       return { action: "DELETE", table: deleteMatch[1], columns: [], values: [], where, pkValue: undefined };
650:     }
651: 
652:     // SELECT ... FROM <table> [WHERE ...] [ORDER BY ...] [LIMIT ?]
653:     const selectMatch = trimmed.match(/^SELECT\s+.+?\s+FROM\s+(\w+)/i);
654:     if (selectMatch) {
655:       const table = selectMatch[1];
656:       const whereMatch = trimmed.match(/WHERE\s+(.+?)(?:\s+ORDER\s+BY|\s+LIMIT|\s+GROUP\s+BY|$)/i);
657:       const where = whereMatch ? parseWhereClause(whereMatch[1].trim(), 0) : null;
658:       return { action: "SELECT", table, columns: [], values: [], where, pkValue: undefined };
659:     }
660: 
661:     return { action: "UNKNOWN", table: "", columns: [], values: [], where: null, pkValue: undefined };
662:   }
663: 
664:   function parseWhereClause(clause: string, offset: number): { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] | null {
665:     // Parse "col = ?" or "col = ? AND col2 = ?" etc.
666:     const conditions: { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] = [];
667:     const parts = clause.split(/\s+(?:AND|OR)\s+/i);
668:     let argIdx = offset;
669:     for (const part of parts) {
670:       const m = part.match(/(\w+)\s*(=|!=|>|<|>=|<=|LIKE)\s*\?/i);
671:       if (m) {
672:         conditions.push({ col: m[1], op: m[2], argIdx });
673:         argIdx++;
674:       }
675:     }
676:     return conditions.length ? conditions : null;
677:   }
678: 
679:   function matchesWhere(row: Record<string, unknown>, where: { col: string; op: string; argIdx: number }[] | null, args: unknown[]): boolean {
680:     if (!where || where.length === 0) return true;
681:     return where.every((w) => {
682:       const val = row[w.col];
683:       const arg = args[w.argIdx];
684:       switch (w.op.toUpperCase()) {
685:         case "=": return val == arg;
686:         case "!=": return val != arg;
687:         case ">": return Number(val) > Number(arg);
688:         case "<": return Number(val) < Number(arg);
689:         case ">=": return Number(val) >= Number(arg);
690:         case "<=": return Number(val) <= Number(arg);
691:         case "LIKE":
692:           const pattern = String(arg).replace(/%/g, ".*").replace(/_/g, ".");
693:           return new RegExp(`^${pattern}$`, "i").test(String(val ?? ""));
694:         default: return false;
695:       }
696:     });
697:   }
698: 
699:   return { prepare, exec, pragma } as SqliteDb;
700: }

(End of file - total 700 lines)
</content>

// @ts-nocheck
/**
 * BAZventures — Single source of truth database.
 *
 * Lives at /home/uzer/baz/data/baz.db. Auto-creates the schema on first boot.
 * WAL mode for concurrent reads + safe writes.
 *
 * Tables (10):
 *   - users            auth + role + team membership
 *   - orgs             agencies / partner orgs that operate BAZventures
 *   - leads            inbound prospects from /contact + /brief forms
 *   - customers        signed clients
 *   - subscriptions    recurring engagements (linked to customers + tiers)
 *   - invoices         issued invoices
 *   - projects         active engagements (linked to customers)
 *   - feedback         client testimonial submissions
 *   - feedback_requests scheduled feedback collection (with public tokens)
 *   - ai_jobs          AI usage tracking (cost + tokens)
 *   - sessions         auth tokens
 *   - audit            append-only mutation log

state.output:
<path>/home/uzer/baz/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # BAZventures — Design System
2: 
3: The public site is **black & white by design**. Color is a single, deliberate layer an expert adds later — never a crutch. This document is the map of the foundation: the token architecture, the shade ramps, the type scale, the mode stance, and the one file that controls color.
4: 
5: > **Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT
6: > **Mode:** dark-only (pure darkness — see §6) · **Corners:** square (only `rounded-full` for pills) · **Fonts:** Outfit / Poppins / JetBrains Mono
7: 
8: ---
9: 
10: ## 1. The one thing to know first
11: **Color is controlled by a single file: `app/color-layer.css`.** It is imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx`, so it always wins. To re-add color, change two numbers there:
12: 
13: ```css
14: --seed-hue: 187;  --seed-sat: 90%;   /* electric cyan  */
15: --seed-hue: 42;   --seed-sat: 85%;   /* stitch gold    */
16: ```
17: 
18: The seed recolors **everything** coherently: the primary ramp, the neutral ramp, borders, the Aether dark-surface ramp, the text ramp, and the branding gradients/glow. Functional **state** colors (success/warning/danger/info) have their own hue/sat knobs in the same file. One file, two numbers for the brand — zero rework elsewhere.
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## 2. The 4-layer CSS architecture
23: Imported in this order in `app/layout.tsx` (last wins on equal specificity):
24: 
25: | # | File | Role |
26: |---|---|---|
27: | 1 | `app/globals.css` | Semantic Tailwind-compat tokens (`--brand`, `--accent`, `--background`, `--foreground`, …), type vars, global reset (`* { border-radius: 0 }`), base element styles, `.text-gradient`. |
28: | 2 | `app/aether-theme.css` | "The Beauty of Darkness" — the Aether darkness ramp, text ramp, spacing/typography/radius/blur/shadow/z/motion (all Fibonacci), glass panels, M2/M3 utilities, print rules. **Hardcoded `hue 42` in its `:root`** — overridden by layer 4. |
29: | 3 | `app/aether-monochrome.css` | The Æther algorithmic token system: the **seed** (`--seed-hue/sat/lum`), the derived `--color-primary-*` and `--color-neutral-*` ramps, semantic aliases (`--fg/--bg/--border/…`), plus a `:root:not(.dark)` light block (disabled — see §6). |
30: | 4 | `app/color-layer.css` | **The control layer (imported last → wins).** Locks the seed to greyscale, drives the Aether ramp + gradients + glow from the seed, neutralizes state colors, and exposes the expert knobs. |
31: 
32: **Rule:** never hardcode color in components. Use the Tailwind semantic classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`, `text-success`, …) which resolve to these tokens. The only file that should define color values is `color-layer.css`.
33: 
34: ---
35: 
36: ## 3. The seed system
37: One seed (`--seed-hue`, `--seed-sat`, `--seed-lum`) derives the entire palette via HSL math. Change the seed → rebrand the world. Currently `0 / 0% / 50%` = pure greyscale.
38: 
39: - `--color-primary` and `--color-primary-5…90` — lightness ramp from the seed (sat follows `--seed-sat`).
40: - `--color-neutral-0…100` — seed-tinted grays at very low saturation (the structural palette).
41: - `--aether-l0…l7` — 8 darkness layers (l0 = the void, l7 = active), hue from `--seed-hue`, low sat.
42: - `--aether-text-primary/secondary/tertiary/ghost` — 4-level text hierarchy on darkness.
43: - `--aether-gradient-*` + `--aether-shadow-glow` — branding gradients/glow, seed-driven (neutral now).
44: 
45: In B&W mode the whole stack is a coherent grayscale. The expert sets hue/sat and every layer warms/cools together.
46: 
47: ---
48: 
49: ## 4. Shade ramps (current B&W values)
50: All resolve to neutral grays today (seed `0/0`):
51: 
52: | Ramp | Range | Used for |
53: |---|---|---|
54: | `--color-neutral-*` | 0 (99%) → 100 (4%) | page bg, surfaces, borders, text |
55: | `--color-primary-*` | 5 (95%) → 90 (12%) | brand fills, accents, focus rings |
56: | `--aether-l0…l7` | 3.9% → 42% | dark surface elevation (void → active) |
57: | `--aether-text-*` | 98% / 65% / 45% / 30% | text hierarchy on darkness |
58: | `--g0…--g100` | legacy grayscale aliases | backward-compat (see §9) |
59: 
60: **Known gap (§9):** `--g55…--g100` are hardcoded hex (`#adb5bd` → `#ffffff`), not seed-driven. They're light-mode grays, dormant in dark mode. Seed-drive them when light mode is enabled.
61: 
62: ---
63: 
64: ## 5. Typography
65: **Fonts** (loaded in `app/layout.tsx` via `next/font`):
66: - **Outfit** — display/headings (`font-display`)
67: - **Poppins** — body/UI (`font-sans`)
68: - **JetBrains Mono** — code, metrics, tags, labels (`font-mono`)
69: 
70: **Scales in `tailwind.config.ts`** (three coexist — see §9 for consolidation):
71: - `display-2xl / xl / lg / md` — fluid `clamp()` headings (**preferred for new work**)
72: - `hero` / `section` — fluid H1/H2
73: - `fib-micro…fib-xxl` — legacy fixed-px Fibonacci scale (being phased out)
74: - Aether also defines `--aether-text-*` px sizes (Fibonacci) in `aether-theme.css`
75: 
76: **Rule:** headlines bold + tight tracking (`tracking-[-0.02em]`), body clean/legible, mono for numbers/tags/system cues. Authority, not trend-chasing.
77: 
78: ---
79: 
80: ## 6. Light & dark mode
81: **The site is dark-only by design.** `components/ui/ThemeProvider.tsx` sets `forcedTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem={false}`, `themes={["dark"]}`. `app/layout.tsx` hardcodes `className="… dark"` + `data-theme="dark"` + a pre-paint script. `aether-theme.css` is "Pure dark mode. No light mode. Only darkness."
82: 
83: **Light tokens already exist** but are dormant: `aether-monochrome.css` has a `:root:not(.dark)` block that reverses the palette. To enable a real light mode later:
84: 1. In `ThemeProvider.tsx`: drop `forcedTheme`, set `enableSystem={true}`, add `"light"` to `themes`.
85: 2. Remove the forced `dark` class + pre-paint script in `layout.tsx`.
86: 3. Audit the dark-only utilities in `aether-theme.css` (glass panels, M2 "white on dark" overlays, gradient-void) — they assume darkness and will need light equivalents.
87: 4. Seed-drive `--g55…--g100` so the light ramp follows the brand.
88: 
89: This is an expert task — the dark UI kit is deep. Don't enable light mode piecemeal.
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: ## 7. Non-negotiable rules
94: - **No hardcoded color in components.** Use semantic Tailwind classes → tokens → `color-layer.css`.
95: - **Square corners everywhere** (`* { border-radius: 0 }`); `rounded-full` only for pills/badges/avatars.
96: - **B&W by default.** Any new accent must flow through `--brand`/`--accent` (seed-driven) or the state knobs — never a literal hex.
97: - **No fabricated metrics.** Composite proof points are flagged `[replace with real]` until real data replaces them.
98: - **Functional state colors (emerald/amber/red) are kept in the internal Hub/admin/console** — they're affordances, not branding. Don't neutralize them there.
99: 
100: ---
101: 
102: ## 8. File map
103: ```
104: app/layout.tsx              # imports the 4 CSS layers in order; forces dark; fonts
105: app/globals.css             # layer 1 — semantic tokens, reset, base styles
106: app/aether-theme.css        # layer 2 — darkness kit (ramp, glass, M2/M3, motion)
107: app/aether-monochrome.css   # layer 3 — seed system + ramps + (dormant) light block
108: app/color-layer.css         # layer 4 — THE control layer (expert edits here)
109: tailwind.config.ts          # tokens→Tailwind classes, type scale, square radii
110: components/ui/ThemeProvider.tsx  # next-themes, dark-only
111: lib/site.ts                 # brand: name=BAZventures, product=the Hub, socials
112: brand/                      # documented brand (colors.md, typography.md, voice.md, tokens.json)
113: public/favicon.svg          # B&W mark (dark squircle, light B)
114: ```
115: 
116: ---
117: 
118: ## 9. Known gaps — recommended next steps for the expert
119: 1. **Vestigial aether UI kit** (`components/ui/aether-index.tsx`, `components/primitives/*`, `components/structure/*`, `components/DesignSystemChecklist.tsx`) — currently has **zero importers** (dead code) and was the main source of hardcoded gold. Decide: revive under the seed system, or remove.
120: 2. **Consolidate the type scale** — pick one fluid scale (`display-*` + `hero`/`section`) and retire `fib-*` + `--aether-text-*` px sizes after auditing usage.
121: 3. **Seed-drive `--g55…--g100`** (hardcoded light grays) so the light ramp follows the brand when light mode is enabled.
122: 4. **`/brandbook`** still shows the old orange/gold swatch palette — regenerate it from the final chosen palette.
123: 5. **Internal Hub/admin** still says "BAZ" in places and keeps functional status colors — a separate product-names + palette pass (out of scope of the public rebrand).
124: 6. **Social handles/URL** (`@bazagency`, `/baz-agency`, `baz.agency`) intentionally left as-is per founder decision — not a code concern.
125: 
126: ---
127: 
128: *Diagnose first, architect the system, ship it, hold the line on what moves revenue.*

(End of file - total 128 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# BAZventures — Design System

The public site is **black & white by design**. Color is a single, deliberate layer an expert adds later — never a crutch. This document is the map of the foundation: the token architecture, the shade ramps, the type scale, the mode stance, and the one file that controls color.

> **Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT
> **Mode:** dark-only (pure darkness — see §6) · **Corners:** square (only `rounded-full` for pills) · **Fonts:** Outfit / Poppins / JetBrains Mono

---

## 1. The one thing to know first
**Color is controlled by a single file: `app/color-layer.css`.** It is imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx`, so it always wins. To re-add color, change two numbers there:

```css
--seed-hue: 187;  --seed-sat: 90%;   /* electric cyan  */
--seed-hue: 42;   --seed-sat: 85%;   /* stitch gold    */
```

The seed recolors **everything** coherently: the primary ramp, the neutral ramp, borders, the Aether dark-surface ramp, the text ramp, and the branding gradients/glow. Functional **state** colors (success/warning/danger/info) have their own hue/sat knobs in the same file. One file, two numbers for the brand — zero rework elsewhere.

---

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * BAZventures — LLM adapter
3:  * Provider-agnostic. Reads env config at request time.
4:  * Falls back gracefully if no key is set — returns a structured "unconfigured" reply
5:  * so the rest of the app never crashes when AI isn't available.
6:  *
7:  * Supported providers:
8:  *   - Gemini (GEMINI_API_KEY, GEMINI_MODEL) — free tier, 1500 req/min
9:  *   - OpenAI (OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL)
10:  *   - Anthropic (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_MODEL)
11:  *   - Ollama (OLLAMA_HOST, OLLAMA_MODEL) — local
12:  *
13:  * Usage:
14:  *   const result = await llm.complete({ prompt, system });
15:  *   if (result.ok) ... else handle(result.error);
16:  */
17: 
18: export type LlmProvider = "openai" | "anthropic" | "ollama" | "gemini" | "stub";
19: 
20: export interface LlmConfig {
21:   provider: LlmProvider;
22:   model: string;
23:   url: string | null;
24:   key: string | null;
25: }
26: 
27: export interface LlmResult {
28:   ok: boolean;
29:   provider: LlmProvider | null;
30:   model: string | null;
31:   text: string | null;
32:   error?: string;
33:   /** Provider-reported token usage, when available. */
34:   usage?: { input: number; output: number; total: number };
35: }
36: 
37: interface CompleteArgs {
38:   prompt: string;
39:   system?: string;
40:   maxTokens?: number;
41:   temperature?: number;
42:   /** Force a specific provider (bypasses env selection). */
43:   provider?: LlmProvider;
44: }
45: 
46: const PROVIDERS: Record<
47:   Exclude<LlmProvider, "stub">,
48:   { url: string | null; defaultModel: string }
49: > = {
50:   openai: { url: "https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", defaultModel: "gpt-4o-mini" },
51:   anthropic: {
52:     url: "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
53:     defaultModel: "claude-3-5-haiku-latest",
54:   },
55:   ollama: { url: null, defaultModel: "llama3.1" },
56:   gemini: { url: "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta", defaultModel: "gemini-2.5-flash" },
57: };
58: 
59: /**
60:  * Detect the provider from env. Returns null when nothing is configured.
61:  * Force a specific provider by setting AI_PROVIDER=openai|anthropic|ollama|stub.
62:  */
63: export function getLlmConfig(override?: LlmProvider): LlmConfig | null {
64:   const forced = override ?? process.env.AI_PROVIDER?.toLowerCase() as LlmProvider | undefined;
65: 
66:   if (forced === "stub" || (forced === undefined && !hasAnyKey())) {
67:     return null; // stub mode — caller should handle via complete()
68:   }
69:   if (forced === "openai" || (forced === undefined && process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY)) {
70:     return {
71:       provider: "openai",
72:       key: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY!,
73:       model: process.env.OPENAI_MODEL || PROVIDERS.openai.defaultModel,
74:       url: PROVIDERS.openai.url,
75:     };
76:   }
77:   if (forced === "anthropic" || (forced === undefined && process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)) {
78:     return {
79:       provider: "anthropic",
80:       key: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY!,
81:       model: process.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL || PROVIDERS.anthropic.defaultModel,
82:       url: PROVIDERS.anthropic.url,
83:     };
84:   }
85:   if (forced === "gemini" || (forced === undefined && process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY)) {
86:     return {
87:       provider: "gemini",
88:       key: process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY!,
89:       model: process.env.GEMINI_MODEL || PROVIDERS.gemini.defaultModel,
90:       url: PROVIDERS.gemini.url,
91:     };
92:   }
93:   if (forced === "ollama" || (forced === undefined && process.env.OLLAMA_HOST)) {
94:     return {
95:       provider: "ollama",
96:       key: null,
97:       model: process.env.OLLAMA_MODEL || PROVIDERS.ollama.defaultModel,
98:       url: `${process.env.OLLAMA_HOST}/api/chat`,
99:     };
100:   }
101:   return null;
102: }
103: 
104: function hasAnyKey(): boolean {
105:   return Boolean(
106:     process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY || process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY || process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY || process.env.OLLAMA_HOST,
107:   );
108: }
109: 
110: /**
111:  * Send a completion request. Returns a structured result regardless of outcome —
112:  * the caller never has to wrap in try/catch for network errors.
113:  */
114: export async function complete(args: CompleteArgs): Promise<LlmResult> {
115:   const cfg = getLlmConfig(args.provider);
116:   if (!cfg) {
117:     return {
118:       ok: false,
119:       provider: null,
120:       model: null,
121:       text: null,
122:       error: "no_provider_configured",
123:     };
124:   }
125: 
126:   try {
127:     switch (cfg.provider) {
128:       case "openai":
129:         return await callOpenAI(cfg, args);
130:       case "anthropic":
131:         return await callAnthropic(cfg, args);
132:       case "ollama":
133:         return await callOllama(cfg, args);
134:       case "gemini":
135:         return await callGemini(cfg, args);
136:       default:
137:         return {
138:           ok: false,
139:           provider: cfg.provider,
140:           model: cfg.model,
141:           text: null,
142:           error: "unsupported_provider",
143:         };
144:     }
145:   } catch (err) {
146:     return {
147:       ok: false,
148:       provider: cfg.provider,
149:       model: cfg.model,
150:       text: null,
151:       error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : "unknown_error",
152:     };
153:   }
154: }
155: 
156: async function callOpenAI(cfg: LlmConfig, args: CompleteArgs): Promise<LlmResult> {
157:   const r = await fetch(cfg.url!, {
158:     method: "POST",
159:     headers: {
160:       "Content-Type": "application/json",
161:       Authorization: `Bearer ${cfg.key}`,
162:     },
163:     body: JSON.stringify({
164:       model: cfg.model,
165:       messages: [
166:         ...(args.system ? [{ role: "system", content: args.system }] : []),
167:         { role: "user", content: args.prompt },
168:       ],
169:       max_tokens: args.maxTokens ?? 1024,
170:       temperature: args.temperature ?? 0.7,
171:     }),
172:   });
173:   if (!r.ok) {
174:     const body = await r.text().catch(() => "");
175:     return {
176:       ok: false,
177:       provider: "openai",
178:       model: cfg.model,
179:       text: null,
180:       error: `openai_${r.status}: ${body.slice(0, 200)}`,
181:     };
182:   }
183:   const j = await r.json();
184:   const text = j.choices?.[0]?.message?.content ?? null;
185:   const usage = j.usage
186:     ? {
187:         input: j.usage.prompt_tokens ?? 0,
188:         output: j.usage.completion_tokens ?? 0,
189:         total: j.usage.total_tokens ?? 0,
190:       }
191:     : undefined;
192:   return { ok: true, provider: "openai", model: cfg.model, text, usage };
193: }
194: 
195: async function callAnthropic(cfg: LlmConfig, args: CompleteArgs): Promise<LlmResult> {
196:   const r = await fetch(cfg.url!, {
197:     method: "POST",
198:     headers: {
199:       "Content-Type": "application/json",
200:       "x-api-key": cfg.key!,
201:       "anthropic-version": "2023-06-01",
202:     },
203:     body: JSON.stringify({
204:       model: cfg.model,
205:       max_tokens: args.maxTokens ?? 1024,
206:       temperature: args.temperature ?? 0.7,
207:       system: args.system,
208:       messages: [{ role: "user", content: args.prompt }],
209:     }),
210:   });
211:   if (!r.ok) {
212:     const body = await r.text().catch(() => "");
213:     return {
214:       ok: false,
215:       provider: "anthropic",
216:       model: cfg.model,
217:       text: null,
218:       error: `anthropic_${r.status}: ${body.slice(0, 200)}`,
219:     };
220:   }
221:   const j = await r.json();
222:   const text = j.content?.[0]?.text ?? null;
223:   const usage = j.usage
224:     ? {
225:         input: j.usage.input_tokens ?? 0,
226:         output: j.usage.output_tokens ?? 0,
227:         total: (j.usage.input_tokens ?? 0) + (j.usage.output_tokens ?? 0),
228:       }
229:     : undefined;
230:   return { ok: true, provider: "anthropic", model: cfg.model, text, usage };
231: }
232: 
233: async function callOllama(cfg: LlmConfig, args: CompleteArgs): Promise<LlmResult> {
234:   const r = await fetch(cfg.url!, {
235:     method: "POST",
236:     headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
237:     body: JSON.stringify({
238:       model: cfg.model,
239:       stream: false,
240:       messages: [
241:         ...(args.system ? [{ role: "system", content: args.system }] : []),
242:         { role: "user", content: args.prompt },
243:       ],
244:       options: {
245:         num_predict: args.maxTokens ?? 1024,
246:         temperature: args.temperature ?? 0.7,
247:       },
248:     }),
249:   });
250:   if (!r.ok) {
251:     return {
252:       ok: false,
253:       provider: "ollama",
254:       model: cfg.model,
255:       text: null,
256:       error: `ollama_${r.status}`,
257:     };
258:   }
259:   const j = await r.json();
260:   const text = j.message?.content ?? null;
261:   return { ok: true, provider: "ollama", model: cfg.model, text };
262: }
263: 
264: async function callGemini(cfg: LlmConfig, args: CompleteArgs): Promise<LlmResult> {
265:   const url = `${cfg.url}/models/${cfg.model}:generateContent?key=${cfg.key}`;
266:   const contents: Array<{ role: string; parts: Array<{ text: string }> }> = [];
267:   if (args.system) {
268:     contents.push({ role: "user", parts: [{ text: args.system }] });
269:     contents.push({ role: "model", parts: [{ text: "Understood." }] });
270:   }
271:   contents.push({ role: "user", parts: [{ text: args.prompt }] });
272: 
273:   const r = await fetch(url, {
274:     method: "POST",
275:     headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
276:     body: JSON.stringify({
277:       contents,
278:       generationConfig: {
279:         maxOutputTokens: args.maxTokens ?? 1024,
280:         temperature: args.temperature ?? 0.7,
281:       },
282:     }),
283:   });
284:   if (!r.ok) {
285:     const body = await r.text().catch(() => "");
286:     return {
287:       ok: false,
288:       provider: "gemini",
289:       model: cfg.model,
290:       text: null,
291:       error: `gemini_${r.status}: ${body.slice(0, 200)}`,
292:     };
293:   }
294:   const j = await r.json();
295:   const text = j.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.[0]?.text ?? null;
296:   const usage = j.usageMetadata
297:     ? {
298:         input: j.usageMetadata.promptTokenCount ?? 0,
299:         output: j.usageMetadata.candidatesTokenCount ?? 0,
300:         total: j.usageMetadata.totalTokenCount ?? 0,
301:       }
302:     : undefined;
303:   return { ok: true, provider: "gemini", model: cfg.model, text, usage };
304: }
305: 
306: /** Returns the current provider name (or 'stub' if no keys) — used by /admin/monitors. */
307: export function llmStatus(): {
308:   provider: LlmProvider | "unconfigured";
309:   model: string | null;
310:   keysPresent: Record<string, boolean>;
311: } {
312:   const cfg = getLlmConfig();
313:   return {
314:     provider: cfg?.provider ?? "unconfigured",
315:     model: cfg?.model ?? null,
316:     keysPresent: {
317:       openai: Boolean(process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY),
318:       anthropic: Boolean(process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY),
319:       ollama: Boolean(process.env.OLLAMA_HOST),
320:       gemini: Boolean(process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY),
321:     },
322:   };
323: }
324: 

(End of file - total 324 lines)
</content>

1: -- BAZ Marketing Agency — RLS Policies
2: -- Run this in the Supabase SQL Editor after the initial schema migration.
3: 
4: -- ── Leads: public can INSERT (contact form), operators can read/write ──
5: CREATE POLICY "Public can submit leads" ON leads
6:   FOR INSERT TO anon, authenticated WITH CHECK (true);
7: 
8: CREATE POLICY "Operators can read leads" ON leads
9:   FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
10: 
11: CREATE POLICY "Operators can update leads" ON leads
12:   FOR UPDATE TO authenticated USING (true);
13: 
14: CREATE POLICY "Operators can delete leads" ON leads
15:   FOR DELETE TO authenticated USING (true);
16: 
17: -- ── Users: users can read their own profile, operators can read all ──
18: CREATE POLICY "Users can read own profile" ON users
19:   FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (auth.uid() = id::uuid OR true);
20: 
21: CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile" ON users
22:   FOR UPDATE TO authenticated USING (auth.uid() = id::uuid);
23: 
24: -- ── All other tables: authenticated operators get full access ──
25: -- (The service_role already bypasses RLS, so this covers the supabase-js client)
26: 
27: -- Customers
28: CREATE POLICY "Operators can read customers" ON customers FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
29: CREATE POLICY "Operators can write customers" ON customers FOR ALL TO authenticated USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
30: 
31: -- Projects
32: CREATE POLICY "Operators can read projects" ON projects FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
33: CREATE POLICY "Operators can write projects" ON projects FOR ALL TO authenticated USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
34: 
35: -- Subscriptions
36: CREATE POLICY "Operators can read subscriptions" ON subscriptions FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
37: CREATE POLICY "Operators can write subscriptions" ON subscriptions FOR ALL TO authenticated USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
38: 
39: -- Invoices
40: CREATE POLICY "Operators can read invoices" ON invoices FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true);
41: CREATE POLICY "Operators can write invoices" ON invoices FOR ALL TO authenticated USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
42: 
43: -- Orgs
